slang: All that and a bag of chips
concerns: Post-Cold War geopolitical shifts and anti-Western sentiment
Economic recovery and stability in emerging markets like China
The ongoing HIV/AIDS epidemic, despite public education efforts
Increasing concerns over school violence and social alienation
The early stages of the 'digital divide' regarding internet access
aspirations: Gaining internet access for personal use at home
Achieving career success in a seemingly stable global economy
Expressing individuality through alternative music and fashion
Experiencing a sense of global community and multicultural understanding

fashion.men: Men's fashion often featured baggy jeans, flannel shirts, and oversized t-shirts, heavily influenced by grunge and hip-hop culture. Sportswear, including tracksuits and branded athletic gear, also became a prominent casual staple for everyday wear.
fashion.women: Women's fashion ranged from minimalist slip dresses and crop tops to grunge-inspired ripped jeans and oversized sweaters. Platform shoes, chokers, and cargo pants were also popular trends, alongside preppy styles like plaid skirts.

failed_products.name: Apple Newton
failed_products.why_failed: It was ahead of its time, expensive, and suffered from clunky handwriting recognition and limited battery life.
failed_products.modern_gap: No, the concept of a personal digital assistant was vastly improved and superseded by smartphones.

failed_products.name: Virtual Boy
failed_products.why_failed: Suffered from monochromatic red display, caused eye strain, lacked immersive games, and was marketed poorly.
failed_products.modern_gap: No, modern VR headsets offer full color and far superior immersion, rendering the Virtual Boy's niche obsolete.

failed_products.name: Circuit City DIVX Disposable DVD
failed_products.why_failed: DIVX discs (1998) cost -5 each and expired after 48 hours of first play, requiring a modem-connected player and monthly fees to reactivate. Consumers revolted publicly, retailers chose DVD, and Circuit City lost 14 million writing off the system in 1999.
failed_products.modern_gap: None. Streaming services solved disposable rental perfectly — but DIVX was the first attempt at DRM-gated consumption and got everything wrong.

ebay_hot_now: Vintage band t-shirts (e.g., Nirvana, Pearl Jam)
Original Pokémon trading card sets (Base Set, Jungle, Fossil)
ebay_sleepers: Rare VHS tapes (limited editions, cult classics)
Early CD boomboxes and portable CD players (for retro aesthetic)
Pogs (less common complete sets or slammer collections)

character_voice.worldview: People from the 90s generally viewed the world with a sense of cautious optimism, shaped by the end of the Cold War and the perceived rise of global peace and prosperity for many Western countries. There was a growing awareness of diverse cultures and a burgeoning belief in individual expression through new media.
character_voice.speech_patterns: Their speech often incorporated irony, popular culture references, and a laid-back, sometimes sarcastic tone, frequently using new slang.
taboo_topics: Discussing personal finances or salary openly

story_accuracy: Ubiquitous and fast internet access or reliance on smartphones (dial-up was common, cell phones were basic and expensive).
People casually discussing very personal topics like mental health or sexuality as openly as in modern times.
Modern social media platforms like Instagram or TikTok being used for communication or influence.
